data.description:
## Context

Placeholder epic from the 2026-07-08 audit follow-up (OSS finding #1: no automated dependency updates; see epic heedless-caloric-pikeperch for the rest of the audit remediation). Stock Dependabot cannot merge here — GitHub is a read-only mirror and code lands only through Gerrit. Research into Gerrit-gated OSS projects (Chromium AutoRoll, Wikimedia LibUp, OpenStack proposal bot, wmde's Dependabot->Gerrit bridge) converged on self-hosted **Renovate with `platform: gerrit`** (native since 37.112.0, still experimental) as the purpose-built option: it pushes each update to refs/for/main, manages Change-Ids via Renovate-Branch commit footers (re-pushes become patchsets, not duplicates), and needs only Contributor push rights — the existing LLM-Review + Verified gates apply unchanged, no autoApprove.

## What (rough shape — to be designed before promotion)

- **Hosting:** run Renovate CLI on a schedule (systemd timer or cron) on the **same AWS server that hosts Gerrit and the Rebar MCP server** — it already holds Gerrit credentials, network adjacency, and a rebar install with the environment signing key.
- **Identity:** a dedicated Gerrit bot account (or RebarBotNava) with HTTP token auth (RENOVATE_PASSWORD), Contributors group, push-for-review only — no self-approve, no vote rights; every bump passes both gates like any change.
- **Commit-ticket trailer integration (the rebar-specific piece):** CI's Verified gate rejects commits whose message doesn't reference a resolving rebar ticket, and Renovate owns the commit-footer area (custom trailers via commitBody don't work on the Gerrit platform). Decision per audit follow-up: a wrapper step around each Renovate run (or a per-update hook) uses the **rebar install on the same server** to `rebar create` a tracking ticket for the dependency change and **immediately force-close it** (`--force-close` — the goal is tracking/provenance, not gate validation; force-closed = deliberately unsigned), then injects that ticket id into the commit via the accepted `<id>:` subject-prefix form (e.g. commitMessagePrefix), since the trailer footer is unavailable. The ticket description records package, from->to versions, and the Renovate change URL.
- **Complementary signal:** keep GitHub Dependabot **security alerts** enabled on the mirror as an independent CVE feed (alerts only, no PRs).
- **Fallback:** if the experimental Gerrit platform proves too rough, replace with a small scheduled bump-and-push job (uv lock --upgrade + push to refs/for/main) reusing the same ticket-creation wrapper.

## Open design questions (resolve before promoting idea -> open)

- One standing ticket per run vs one ticket per dependency change (leaning per-change for provenance granularity)
- Whether the subject-prefix `<id>:` form satisfies verify.require_ticket_for_commit as-is for Renovate-authored subjects, or the config needs a bot exemption
- Renovate config scope: pip (pyproject core + extras), github-actions manager for the workflow pins (pairs with the audit's SHA-pinning recommendation), grouping/schedule/noise budget
- Secret handling for the bot HTTP token on the server (align with the SSM/ephemeral-secrets pattern, ADR-0008)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Renovate runs on a schedule on the Gerrit/MCP AWS server with platform: gerrit against this repo
- [ ] Each dependency update lands as a normal Gerrit change that passes LLM-Review + Verified with no human-side ceremony beyond review
- [ ] Every Renovate change carries a resolving rebar ticket id (created + force-closed automatically on the server) satisfying the commit-ticket gate
- [ ] Dependabot security alerts remain enabled on the mirror as an independent signal
- [ ] Runbook documented (docs/) covering the bot account, token rotation, pausing the roller, and the fallback path
data.title: Stand up self-hosted Renovate (platform: gerrit) on the Gerrit/MCP AWS server, with rebar-ticket trailer integration
